expressions.notes: -(으)로 시간 = time to + V; time for + V-ing

expressions.english: to be good at studying; to have good grades in school
expressions.notes: 잘하다 = to be good at something

expressions.english: to be bad at studying; to have bad grades in school
expressions.notes: 못하다 = to be bad/poor at something

expressions.english: What are you studying these days?
I have too much to study.
expressions.notes:
-(으)로 게 = to + V (subject)
-(으)로 게 is the contracted form of -(으)로 것이

expressions.english: Since it's noisy here, I can't study.
expressions.notes:
시끄럽다 = to be noisy, loud
-아/어/서 = because, since
안 되다 = S + doesn't work; can't + V

expressions.english: No matter how much you study, there's always more (to study).
expressions.notes:
-아/어/도 = even though; no matter how much + S + V
끝 = end, finish
끝이 없다 = there is no end; to never end

expressions.english: If you study alone, it's easy to give up.
expressions.notes:
혼자 = alone, by oneself
-(으)면 = if + S + V; when/once + S + V
포기하다 = to give up
-기 쉽다 = to be easy to + V

expressions.korean: 이 책은 공부에 도움이 많이 돼요.
expressions.english: This book helps a lot with studying.
expressions.notes:
이 = this
책 = book
도움 = help
도움이 되다 = to be helpful

expressions.korean: 저는 학교 다닐 때 공부를 못했어요.
expressions.english: When I was going to school, I was not a good student.
expressions.notes:
저 = (polite) I; me
학교 = school
다니다 = to go to (a place); to attend
-(으)ㄹ 때 = when/while + S + V

그 = the; that
외국 = foreign country
-고 오다 = to go do something and then come back

expressions.korean: 엄마가 방에 들어와서 공부하는 척 했어요.
expressions.english: My mom came into my room, so I pretended to be studying.
expressions.notes:
엄마 = mom
방 = room
들어오다 = to come in
-는 척 하다 = to pretend

expressions.korean: 어제 공부한 내용이 하나도 기억이 안 나요.
expressions.english: I can't remember anything I studied yesterday.
expressions.notes:
어제 = yesterday
내용 = content; material
하나 = one (native Korean number)
하나도 = not even one; not at all
기억 = memory
기억이 나다 = to remember

expressions.english: The more you study the Korean language, the more fun it is.
expressions.notes:
-(으)면 -(으)ㄹ수록 = the more ..., the more ....
재미있다 = to be fun; to be funny; to be interesting

expressions.korean: 어제 밤을 새워서 공부했더니 너무 피곤해요.
expressions.english: I stayed up all night studying, so I am really exhausted.
expressions.notes:
밤 = night
밤을 새우다 = to stay up all night
-았/었기 때문에 = since/because + I/we + V-ed
피곤하다 = to be tired, exhausted; to be tiring
